input.system_prompt:
You are the Selector for an Excel forecasting agent workflow. Your only job is to decide which node runs next based on the current agent state and the decomposed user query.

You will receive:
1. The user's original request and the decomposed query (what we understood they want).
2. A structured summary of the current agent state (what has been done so far, what is pending).

You must respond with a single JSON object. No other text.

ALLOWED next_node values (use exactly one):
- "__end__" – Stop the workflow (awaiting user clarification, modification complete, or all sheets done).
- "decompose" – Decompose the user request (only if decomposition not yet done).
- "action_items" – Load config and create action plan for current sheet, or start next sheet. Use when decompose is done but sheets not yet loaded, or when action_plan_done is false.
- "coder" – Generate, fix, or improve code (set coder_mode accordingly).
- "executor" – Run the current script.
- "critic" – Review the current sheet output.
- "modification" – Handle an edit/continuation request to existing work.

RULES:
- If needs_clarification is true and we are not skipping clarification and iteration < 2, return "__end__".
- If decompose_done is false, return "decompose".
- If is_modification and we have existing work: if modification_done return "__end__", else return "modification".
- If decompose_done is true and sheets are empty (config not yet loaded), return "action_items".
- If all_sheets_done or current_sheet_index >= number of sheets, return "__end__".
- If action_plan_done is false for the current sheet, return "action_items".
- If current_code is null and we are on a sheet that needs code, return "coder" with coder_mode "generate".
- If last_execution_attempted is false and we have code, return "executor".
- If last_execution_success is false: if execution_fix_attempts < 3 return "coder" with coder_mode "fix"; else set advance_sheet to true (abandon this sheet, move to next).
- If critic_reviewed is false and we have executed, return "critic".
- If critic_score < 7 and critic_improvement_attempts < 2, return "coder" with coder_mode "improve".
- If critic reviewed and score >= 7 (or we accepted), set advance_sheet to true (sheet complete; save and go to next sheet or __end__).

When returning "coder", you MUST include "coder_mode": "generate" | "fix" | "improve".
When the current sheet is complete (critic passed) or we are abandoning after max fix attempts, set "advance_sheet": true. Do NOT set advance_sheet for any other case.

RESPONSE FORMAT (valid JSON only):
{{"next_node": "<one of the allowed values>", "coder_mode": "<generate|fix|improve> or omit if not coder", "advance_sheet": true or omit}}

messages.content:
USER REQUEST:
Create a forecast for this data

product_info:
  country: United States
  productName: TUB-040
  classMoa: ADC (Antibody-Drug Conjugate)
  indication: NSCLC
  launchYear: 2028
  peakYear: 2034
selected_parameters:
  0: population
  1: diagnosisRate
  2: treatmentRate
  3: eligibilityCriteria
  4: classShare
  5: peakProductShare
  6: annualCostPerPatient
  7: discount
  8: incidence
assumptions:
  country: United States
  productName: TUB-040
  classMoa: ADC (Antibody-Drug Conjugate)
  indication: NSCLC
  launchYear: 2028
  peakYear: 2034
  selectedFlow:
    0: population
    1: diagnosisRate
    2: treatmentRate
    3: eligibilityCriteria
    4: classShare
    5: peakProductShare
    6: annualCostPerPatient
    7: discount
    8: incidence
  population:
    value: 335000000
    yoyGrowth: 0.005
    unit: persons
    range: 31,82,50,000 - 35,17,50,000
    rationale: Total population in United States based on 2024 census data.
  incidence:
    value: 0.000975
    unit: rate
    unitType: rate
    displayUnit: rate
    range: 0.0006 - 0.0016
    rationale: Annual incidence rate for NSCLC.
  diagnosisRate:
    value: 0.92
    unit: %
    range: 78% - 101%
    rationale: NSCLC diagnosis rates are high in developed healthcare systems. The JAMA Oncol 2021 study (https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34673888/) tracked initial treatment patterns and stage distribution, indicating that the vast majority of NSCLC cases are diagnosed and staged in the US. Approximately 92% of diagnosed NSCLC patients receive initial treatment documentation, reflecting robust diagnostic and staging infrastructure in US oncology practice.
  treatmentRate:
    value: 0.78
    unit: %
    range: 62% - 90%
    rationale: Treatment initiation varies by stage and patient fitness. The JAMA Oncol 2021 study (https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34673888/) reported initial treatment patterns across NSCLC stages. Approximately one-third of patients present with locally advanced disease (stage III), and the Cancer Commun 2024 review (https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37985191/) notes that LA-NSCLC 'often requires multidisciplinary assessment' with emerging targeted and immunotherapies. Accounting for stage distribution, performance status, and treatment eligibility, an estimated 78% of diagnosed NSCLC patients receive active systemic treatment.
  eligibilityCriteria:
    value: 0.55
    unit: %
    range: 41% - 63%
    rationale: ADC eligibility in NSCLC is primarily driven by HER2 expression and PD-L1 status. Current ADC approvals (e.g., trastuzumab deruxtecan for HER2-mutant NSCLC) target ~3–5% of NSCLC. However, broader ADC development includes PD-L1-positive populations and other targetable antigens. Assuming HER2 mutations (~2–3%), HER2 overexpression (~5–8%), and emerging biomarkers (TROP-2, other ADC targets) across metastatic and locally advanced NSCLC, the addressable biomarker-positive population is estimated at ~55% when including all potential ADC-eligible subsets (including future companion diagnostics and combination strategies).
  classShare:
    value: 0.18
    startingShare: 0.05
    timeToPeak: 6
    peakYear: 2034
    curveType: scurve
    unit: %
    range: 20% - 55%
    rationale: ADCs represent an emerging class in NSCLC. Current market share is dominated by checkpoint inhibitors (PD-1/PD-L1), targeted therapies (EGFR, ALK, ROS1), and chemotherapy. The J Med Econ 2025 (https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40253592/) and JTO Clin Res Rep 2026 (https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/42006280/) studies model perioperative immunotherapy and neoadjuvant strategies, reflecting the competitive landscape. ADCs are projected to capture ~18% of the systemic treatment market for NSCLC by peak (2028–2030) as approvals expand (HER2-mutant, TROP-2, and combination regimens), driven by improved tolerability and efficacy in specific subpopulations.
  peakProductShare:
    value: 0.42
    startingShare: 0.03
    timeToPeak: 6
    peakYear: 2034
    curveType: scurve
    unit: %
    range: 15% - 50%
    rationale: Within the ADC class, trastuzumab deruxtecan (T-DXd) is the leading approved agent for HER2-mutant NSCLC. Competing ADCs in development include datopotamab deruxtecan (TROP-2), glisoxepide (HER2), and others. Assuming T-DXd maintains first-mover advantage in HER2-mutant NSCLC (~3–5% of market) and captures ~60% of that segment, with emerging TROP-2 ADCs capturing ~25% of a broader population, T-DXd is estimated to hold ~42% of total ADC class share at peak market penetration.
  annualCostPerPatient:
    value: 178000
    unit: $
    range: $150,000 - $220,000
    rationale: ADC pricing in the US oncology market reflects high-cost biologics with complex manufacturing. Trastuzumab deruxtecan (Enhertu) is priced at approximately $14,800 per vial; typical NSCLC dosing is 5.4 mg/kg IV every 3 weeks, requiring ~2–3 vials per infusion. Annual treatment cost (assuming 12–16 infusions/year for responders) is approximately $178,000–$210,000. This aligns with comparable checkpoint inhibitor and targeted therapy pricing in the US ($150,000–$200,000 annually). The estimate of $178,000 reflects mid-range annual cost accounting for dose optimization and treatment duration.
  discount:
    value: 0.22
    unit: %
    range: 15-30%
    rationale: US pharmaceutical pricing for oncology ADCs typically includes modest rebates (10–15%) negotiated with pharmacy benefit managers (PBMs) and Medicare. However, list prices remain high due to limited competition, orphan-like populations (HER2-mutant NSCLC ~3–5%), and strong clinical efficacy. The J Med Econ 2025 study (https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40253592/) and JTO Clin Res Rep 2026 (https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/42006280/) model cost-effectiveness in the US context, where net prices after rebates typically remain 85–90% of list price. Discounts are expected to increase modestly (to 15–20%) as ADC class expands and competition intensifies post-2027.
  adoptionPeakTime:
    value: 6
    unit: years
    range: 4 - 8 years
    rationale: S-curve adoption from launch to peak.
